next_head_excerpt:
# Chapter 6

The next evening, twenty-four hours after the Bretz operation had run long and cold and left Hartig's name stuck to the back of her teeth, Anya hit the landing outside her parents' apartment and paused with the spare key between her fingers. Yor was cooking. Garlic, sugar, butter, and heat had pushed past partnership into open hostility, a scorched sweetness that stuck to the stairwell walls and would probably still be there tomorrow.

She'd spent the day at her cover office moving paper from one pile to another and pretending she wasn't still hearing Damian on the park bench, still feeling the shape of his hand from the walk back to the car, still running Hartig through every corridor of her head until the name stopped sounding like a person and started sounding like a door. None of it had worked. By then she was tired enough for home to feel less like a comfort than a place where she had to keep too many parts of herself from spilling.

Anya let herself in with the spare key she'd carried since she was nine. The apartment hit her the way it always did: warm, cluttered, faintly of Bond's fur beneath whatever Yor had done to the kitchen. Loid's shoes were lined up by the door, toes to the wall, exactly parallel. Yor's were kicked off at different angles, one on its side. Bond was asleep on the couch with his head hanging off the armrest, white fur covering the cushions in the thin permanent layer no amount of vacuuming ever fully beat. A grocery sack sat by the kitchen entrance with leeks sticking out of the top. Someone had meant well. Someone else had added h...
open_hooks_to_carry: The undercover-couple dynamic and Damian's panicked interior monologue
Comic cascading failures: the dog, the mistaken identity, the improvised recovery
Anya extracting the target name from Colonel Bretz's thoughts mid-conversation
Post-mission ease — the first moment of genuine warmth between them
'Same laugh' moment escalated: Damian's childhood memory nearly surfaces fully — a specific girl, a specific schoolyard, a specific afternoon

payoffs_to_land.description: The 'same laugh' moment escalates the recognition — Damian is thinking of a specific childhood memory that nearly surfaces completely

prev_tail_excerpt:
...mian could probably get more through his own side. Still useful.

She did not send a second note. One was enough to damn herself with.

Instead she drafted a harmless follow-up event brief for the mission files in case anybody later asked why she cared about the Gesellschaft dinner at all. Cover maintenance. Not elegant. Necessary.

By the time she finished, the typewriter down the hall had stopped. The clerk thoughts had thinned. Somewhere in the building a cleaner's bucket rattled over tile. Same sound as the ministry corridor, somehow.

Anya checked the wall clock. Past eight.

No reply would come tonight through official channels. Good. It was safer that way. She didn't need proof Damian had taken the bait. The envelope existing was already enough.

She closed the binder, locked the notebook in her desk, and sat with both hands flat on the blotter until the tremor settled to something livable.

At the front desk the guard glanced up from his crossword.

"Long night?"

"Apparently culture never sleeps," Anya said.

He groaned in sympathy without really hearing her and went back to his puzzle.

Through the dispatch room window she looked once more at the ministry pouch, sealed for the morning run, squat and ordinary on the shelf, with her envelope somewhere inside it among seating charts, routing slips, and other harmless-looking paperwork.

That was the shape of it now. Not a feeling. Not a line pulling taut in the dark. A sealed pouch, Colonel Bretz's dinner, and Tuesday already moving toward both of them whether that turned out to be clever or suicidal or far too late.
secondary_character_beats: Colonel Bretz should feel like a real person with a sciatica problem and family resentments, not a faceless intelligence target
state_deltas_required: first joint operation requiring them to pose as a couple and extract intelligence from a military officer at a private club
First successful collaboration. Their working dynamic crystallizes — bickering competence, comic friction, mutual respect. The recognition fuse burns closer.
